Additionally, Twilio participates in the E-Verify program in certain locations, as required by law.</p></div>

jobs.title: Salesforce Technical Administrator
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Who we are&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>At Twilio, we’re shaping the future of communications, all from the comfort of our homes. We deliver innovative solutions to<a class="c-link" href="https://customers.twilio.com/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://customers.twilio.com/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">&nbsp;hundreds of thousands of businesses&nbsp;</a>and empower millions of developers worldwide to craft personalized customer experiences.</p> <p>Our dedication to <a class="c-link" href="https://www.twilio.com/en-us/blog/open-work" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.twilio.com/en-us/blog/open-work" data-sk="tooltip_parent">remote-first work</a>, and strong culture of connection and global inclusion means that no matter your location, you’re part of a vibrant team with diverse experiences making a global impact each day. As we continue to revolutionize how the world interacts, we’re acquiring new skills and experiences that make work feel truly rewarding. Your career at Twilio is in your hands.<br><br>We use Artificial Intelligence (AI) to help make our hiring process efficient. That said, every hiring decision is made by real Twilions!</p> <p><span style="color: rgb(255, 255, 255);">.</span></p></div><p><strong>See yourself at Twilio</strong></p> <p>Join the team as Twilio’s next <strong>Salesforce Tech Administrator</strong></p> <p><strong>About the job</strong></p> <p>We are actively recruiting for this role to fill an existing vacancy. This position is needed for someone with a solid understanding of the Salesforce.com platform looking to take the next step in their career and get more exposure into the operational processes that drive systems designs.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <p>As a Salesforce Administrator in our GTM Automation team, you will get to know our unique business processes and how to build and maintain the systems that power them. In this role, you will be responsible for improving our Salesforce.com platform and ensuring data excellence across the organization. You will be challenged to think creatively about how to reshape existing processes to become more efficient and your work will be integral to supporting impactful programs managed by the team to contribute to the success of the sales team and support Twilio’s fast growth.&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>&nbsp;Assist project managers and product Owners&nbsp; in various work streams on Salesforce.com</li> <li>Data management in Salesforce.com and other business systems, including auditing and resolving data integrity at a large scale.</li> <li>Collaborate with CPQ and Opportunity Life Cycle Management teams&nbsp; to uncover process gaps and develop elegant solutions</li> <li>Work with Opportunity Life Cycle Management/CPQ value streams to deliver the strategic projects</li> <li>Understand and fix existing system problems on a daily basis</li> <li>Recommend and implement process automation and best practices for CPQ and OLM efficiency.</li> <li>Continually evaluate and identify gaps in our existing codebase to develop solutions for increased extensibility.</li> <li>Build dashboards and reports to monitor KPIs across the revenue funnel and support strategic decision-making</li> </ul> <p><strong>Qualifications&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Twilio values diverse experiences from all kinds of industries, and we encourage everyone who meets the required qualifications to apply. If your career is just starting or hasn't followed a traditional path, don't let that stop you from considering Twilio. We are always looking for people who will bring something new to the table!</p> <p><strong>Required:</strong></p> <ul> <li>2-4 years of experience with Salesforce.com implementation (design, configuration, deployment, lightning flows)</li> <li>Must have Conga CPQ or Salesforce CPQ administration experience like Product configuration, PLI creation , Attribute creation, CR creation, etc.,</li> <li>Must have hands on experience building Flow, SOQL, and advanced declarative automation</li> <li>Solid understanding of Salesforce data model, sharing rules, and security architecture.</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to understand business needs and translate requirements into technical solutions</li> <li>Salesforce Administrator certification (ADM-201) required</li> <li>Strong presentation, communication, and facilitation skills</li> <li>Comprehensive experience on debugging and troubleshooting in Salesforce</li> <li>Strong organizational and business analysis skills; detail-oriented and able to work independently, manage and develop smaller scale projects.</li> <li>Knowledge on Salesforce.com configuration best practices</li> </ul> <p><strong>Desired:</strong></p> <ul> <li>1+ year of experience designing or developing on Salesforce (Apex)</li> <li>Conga/ SFDC CPQ specialist Certification</li> <li>​​Salesforce Certified Platform App Builder</li> <li>Familiarity with Apex coding</li> <li>Experienced developing Lightning Components in Salesforce Lightning</li> <li>Excellent communication skills for working with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Location</strong></p> <p>This role will be based remotely in British Colombia, Alberta, or Ontario Canada</p> <p><strong>Travel&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>We prioritize connection and opportunities to build relationships with our customers and each other. For this role, you may be required to travel occasionally to participate in project or team in-person meetings.</p> <p><strong>What We Offer</strong></p> <p>Working at Twilio offers many benefits, including competitive pay, generous time off, ample parental and wellness leave, healthcare, a retirement savings program, and much more. Offerings vary by location.</p> <p>The estimated pay ranges for this role are as follows:</p> <ul> <li><span class="collapsed-field-text">CAD $90,800.00 - CAD $113,500.00</span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>The successful candidate’s starting salary will be determined based on permissible, non-discriminatory factors such as skills, experience, and geographic location.&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Twilio thinks big. Do you?</strong></p> <p>We like to solve problems, take initiative, pitch in when needed, and are always up for trying new things. That's why we seek out colleagues who embody our values — something we call <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/values">Twilio Magic</a>. Additionally, we empower employees to build <a href="https://twilio.org/support-and-resources/impact-fund">positive change in their communities</a> by supporting their volunteering and donation efforts.</p> <p>So, if you're ready to unleash your full potential, do your best work, and be the best version of yourself, apply now! If this role isn't what you're looking for, <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/jobs#open-positions">please consider other open positions.</a></p> <p><strong>Twilio is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.</strong> We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, reproductive health decisions, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, genetic information, political views or activity, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act. Additionally, Twilio participates in the E-Verify program in certain locations, as required by law.</p></div>

jobs.title: Software Engineer
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Who we are&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>At Twilio, we’re shaping the future of communications, all from the comfort of our homes. We deliver innovative solutions to<a class="c-link" href="https://customers.twilio.com/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://customers.twilio.com/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">&nbsp;hundreds of thousands of businesses&nbsp;</a>and empower millions of developers worldwide to craft personalized customer experiences.</p> <p>Our dedication to <a class="c-link" href="https://www.twilio.com/en-us/blog/open-work" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.twilio.com/en-us/blog/open-work" data-sk="tooltip_parent">remote-first work</a>, and strong culture of connection and global inclusion means that no matter your location, you’re part of a vibrant team with diverse experiences making a global impact each day. As we continue to revolutionize how the world interacts, we’re acquiring new skills and experiences that make work feel truly rewarding. Your career at Twilio is in your hands.<br><br>We use Artificial Intelligence (AI) to help make our hiring process efficient. That said, every hiring decision is made by real Twilions!</p> <p><span style="color: rgb(255, 255, 255);">.</span></p></div><p><strong>See yourself at Twilio</strong></p> <p>Join the team as Twilio’s next Software Engineer.</p> <p><strong>About the job</strong></p> <p>This position is needed to build and operate the critical infrastructure that powers webhook delivery across all Twilio products. You'll design features, implement system improvements, and shape the future of this high-scale platform service that dispatches billions of webhooks to customers worldwide.</p> <p>As a backend engineer, you'll develop Java services and manage Kubernetes infrastructure to deliver features and improvements to Twilio Webhooks. Engineering teams at Twilio operate as autonomous mini-startups, giving you ownership and impact.Learn more about Twilio Webhooks: <a href="https://www.twilio.com/docs/usage/webhooks">https://www.twilio.com/docs/usage/webhooks</a></p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <p>In this role, you’ll:</p> <ul> <li>Design, implement, test rigorously and support microservices at scale in a collaborative environment.&nbsp;</li> <li>Advocate for and implement operational best practices and serve on-call for the team’s services.</li> <li>Demonstrate quickness in learning and interest in teaching others. Take part in sprint and quarterly planning ceremonies.</li> <li>Contribute to a highly collaborative team, sharing your ideas and requesting feedback.</li> <li>Communicate complex ideas simply through verbal and written communication. Engineering teams are autonomous mini-startups at Twilio.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Qualifications&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Twilio values diverse experiences from all kinds of industries, and we encourage everyone who meets the required qualifications to apply. If your career is just starting or hasn't followed a traditional path, don't let that stop you from considering Twilio. We are always looking for people who will bring something new to the table!</p> <p><strong>*Required:</strong></p> <ul> <li>2+ years of backend development experience, preferably with Java.</li> <li>Experience consuming and designing mature REST APIs.</li> <li>Previous experience working with Cloud technologies.</li> <li>Demonstrated cross-team communication and collaboration skills.</li> <li>Experience with engineering best practices in all phases of the software development life cycle.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience with or interest in designing and operating reliable, scalable systems.</li> <li>Computer Science degree or equivalent experience.</li> <li>Work experience on a customer-facing product&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Desired:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Proven ability to work carefully and methodically on high-impact, mission-critical systems</li> <li>Experience deploying and operating services on Kubernetes.</li> <li>Experience building fault-tolerant backend services on top of AWS managed services (like databases, caches)</li> <li>Experience working with high traffic, highly scalable systems and complex code bases.</li> <li>Observability tooling (Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, Rollbar…)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Location</strong></p> <p><em>&nbsp;</em>This role will be remote, and based in Spain.</p> <p><strong>Travel&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>We prioritize connection and opportunities to build relationships with our customers and each other. For this role, you may be required to travel occasionally to participate in project or team in-person meetings.</p> <p><strong>What We Offer</strong></p> <p>Working at Twilio offers many benefits, including competitive pay, generous time off, ample parental and wellness leave, healthcare, a retirement savings program, and much more. Offerings vary by location.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Twilio thinks big. Do you?</strong></p> <p>We like to solve problems, take initiative, pitch in when needed, and are always up for trying new things. That's why we seek out colleagues who embody our values — something we call <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/values">Twilio Magic</a>. Additionally, we empower employees to build <a href="https://twilio.org/support-and-resources/impact-fund">positive change in their communities</a> by supporting their volunteering and donation efforts.</p> <p>So, if you're ready to unleash your full potential, do your best work, and be the best version of yourself, apply now! If this role isn't what you're looking for, <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/jobs#open-positions">please consider other open positions.</a></p> <p><strong>Twilio is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.</strong> We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, reproductive health decisions, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, genetic information, political views or activity, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act. Additionally, Twilio participates in the E-Verify program in certain locations, as required by law.</p></div>

jobs.title: Technical Support Engineer 2 (Email Infra)
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Who we are&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>At Twilio, we’re shaping the future of communications, all from the comfort of our homes. We deliver innovative solutions to<a class="c-link" href="https://customers.twilio.com/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://customers.twilio.com/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">&nbsp;hundreds of thousands of businesses&nbsp;</a>and empower millions of developers worldwide to craft personalized customer experiences.</p> <p>Our dedication to <a class="c-link" href="https://www.twilio.com/en-us/blog/open-work" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.twilio.com/en-us/blog/open-work" data-sk="tooltip_parent">remote-first work</a>, and strong culture of connection and global inclusion means that no matter your location, you’re part of a vibrant team with diverse experiences making a global impact each day. As we continue to revolutionize how the world interacts, we’re acquiring new skills and experiences that make work feel truly rewarding. Your career at Twilio is in your hands.<br><br>We use Artificial Intelligence (AI) to help make our hiring process efficient. That said, every hiring decision is made by real Twilions!</p> <p><span style="color: rgb(255, 255, 255);">.</span></p></div><p><strong>See yourself at Twilio</strong></p> <p>Join the team as our next<strong> Technical Support Engineer 2 (Email Infra) - EMEA.</strong></p> <p><strong>About the job</strong></p> <p>This position is needed to support our customers’ use of the Twilio platform, and help customers get the best experience from Twilio’s products. You will function as the voice of Twilio to our customers and partners, and operate as a subject matter expert for Twilio’s APIs and SDKs.</p> <p>This role reports to a Manager, Technical Support and will be based in our India office. This position requires working in a shift. The current shift timings for shift based roles supporting our <span style="text-decoration: underline;">EMEA</span> customers is <strong>12pm-9pm</strong> or <strong>1pm-10pm IST</strong>.&nbsp;</p> <p>This position also includes rostering. Rosters will be of a <span style="text-decoration: underline;">5 day</span> work week, and could include one or both weekend days. (<strong>Mon-Fri</strong>, <strong>Tue-Sat</strong> or <strong>Sun-Thu</strong>).</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <p>In this role, you’ll:</p> <ul> <li>Use your strong technical and diplomatic skills to address customer issues and provide customer feedback to Twilio’s Product and Engineering teams.</li> <li>Be ready to assist our customers when they need us. This position may require working an irregular shift, including the weekend (Shifts are subject to change based on evolving team and customer needs).</li> <li>Work with our customers' and partners' developers, architects, and support personnel to resolve complex problems with potentially very costly and far-reaching consequences.</li> <li>Collaborate with your teammates and the Twilio Product and Engineering teams via Slack as well as filing JIRAs to report reproducible bugs.</li> <li>Review and contribute to internal knowledge bases and external technical resources to stay current on industry shifts and standards.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Qualifications&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Twilio values diverse experiences from all kinds of industries, and we encourage everyone who meets the required qualifications to apply. If your career is just starting or hasn't followed a traditional path, don't let that stop you from considering Twilio. We are always looking for people who will bring something new to the table!</p> <p><strong>*Required:</strong></p> <ul> <li>3+ years experience, with deep understanding of <strong>Email infrastructure</strong> such as <em>Mail User Agent </em>(<span style="text-decoration: underline;">MUA</span>) &amp; <em>Message Transfer Agent</em> (<span style="text-decoration: underline;">MTA</span>)</li> <li>Good understanding of protocols - <strong>Encryption-based Internet Security Protocol</strong> such as <em>SSL</em>, <em>TLS &amp;</em>&nbsp;<strong>Communication Protocol</strong> such as <em>SMTP</em>. <ul> <li><span style="text-decoration: underline;">Bonus</span>: Knowledge on <strong>Email protocols</strong> such as <em>POP3</em> and <em>IMAP</em> and <strong>Client-Server protocol</strong> such as <em>Telnet</em> would be a HUGE plus.</li> </ul> </li> <li>Skills in troubleshooting network connectivity issues, with an understanding of TCP/UDP, and basics of SSL/TLS.&nbsp;</li> <li>Good knowledge of RESTful technology, previous work with APIs and ability to understand and solve issues with cloud solutions.</li> <li>High competency in communicating complex technical issues to both technical and non-technical audiences via phone or email mediums.</li> <li>Ability to stand in the customer’s shoes and demonstrated dedication to the customer experience.</li> <li>Excellent written and verbal communication skills.</li> <li>Excellence in task prioritization and evaluation of situational urgency.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Desired:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Advanced time management skills, ability to work well under pressure, and proficiency at developing workflows to increase troubleshooting efficiency while still following standard processes and procedures.</li> <li>Ability to influence and build effective working relationships with all levels of the organization.</li> <li>Interest in utilizing customer feedback to identify and drive improvements in our products.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li>Basic understanding of Curl command, Webhook.</li> <li>Basic SQL knowledge to query data from snowflake.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Location</strong></p> <p>This role will be <span style="text-decoration: underline;">remote</span>, and based in either <strong>Karnataka</strong>, <strong>Maharashtra</strong>, <strong>Telangana</strong>, <strong>Tamil Nadu</strong> or <strong>New Delhi</strong>.</p> <p><strong>Travel&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>We prioritize connection and opportunities to build relationships with our customers and each other. For this role, you may be required to travel occasionally to participate in project or team in-person meetings.</p> <p><strong>What We Offer</strong></p> <p>Working at Twilio offers many benefits, including competitive pay, generous time off, ample parental and wellness leave, healthcare, a retirement savings program, and much more. Offerings vary by location.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Twilio thinks big. Do you?</strong></p> <p>We like to solve problems, take initiative, pitch in when needed, and are always up for trying new things. That's why we seek out colleagues who embody our values — something we call <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/values">Twilio Magic</a>. Additionally, we empower employees to build <a href="https://twilio.org/support-and-resources/impact-fund">positive change in their communities</a> by supporting their volunteering and donation efforts.</p> <p>So, if you're ready to unleash your full potential, do your best work, and be the best version of yourself, apply now! If this role isn't what you're looking for, <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/jobs#open-positions">please consider other open positions.</a></p> <p><strong>Twilio is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.</strong> We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, reproductive health decisions, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, genetic information, political views or activity, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act. Additionally, Twilio participates in the E-Verify program in certain locations, as required by law.</p></div>

jobs.title: Revenue Operations Analyst II
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Affirm is reinventing credit to make it more honest and friendly, giving consumers the flexibility to buy now and pay later without any hidden fees or compounding interest.</p></div><h4><strong>About The Revenue Team&nbsp;</strong></h4> <p>The Revenue team drives Affirm’s top-line growth through strategic partnerships, pricing, and go-to-market execution. We acquire, retain, and expand merchant relationships across eCommerce, in-store, telesales, and the Affirm app. The team leads onboarding, relationship management, and performance optimization to help partners succeed. We deliver value-added solutions, drive adoption, and support long-term business growth by connecting merchants and consumers through Affirm’s products.</p> <p><strong>About The Team</strong></p> <p>We're looking for a curious, driven analyst to join our Revenue Operations team. Revenue Operations serves as the backbone of the Revenue organization at Affirm. We follow a data-driven approach that combines elements of strategy, systems, process, and analytics to align go-to-market motions across the organization.</p> <p>As a Revenue Operations Analyst II, you will be a key partner to our Marketing, Business Development, and early Sales teams - focused on making the top of the funnel predictable, clean, and scalable. You'll design and optimize the processes, automation, and tooling that govern how leads, contacts, and accounts enter the revenue funnel, ensuring downstream teams can execute with confidence. The ideal candidate is equal parts tactical, building automation and workflows, and strategic thinking through lifecycle design, routing logic, and coverage models that scale with the business.</p> <h4>What You'll Do</h4> <ul> <li> <p>Design and optimize top of funnel merchant lifecycle processes — from Marketing touch through Contact creation, Account readiness, and Discovery handoff — with an emphasis on automation, data quality, and clear documentation</p> </li> <li> <p>Own and define lead routing logic, automation triggers, and lifecycle dependencies that govern how leads and accounts move through the top of funnel; partner with Business Systems for implementation</p> </li> <li> <p>Own cross-functional initiatives end to end — managing stakeholders across Sales, Marketing, Partnerships, Legal, Product, Revenue Ops, Analytics, and others</p> </li> <li> <p>Support attribution tracking and funnel conversion analysis for Marketing, BDA, and early funnel performance to surface actionable insights</p> </li> <li> <p>Own workflow automations (e.g., Slack notifications, cross-tool workflows, process triggers) that improve speed-to-lead and reduce manual work</p> </li> <li> <p>Collaborate with the Business Systems team to improve Salesforce and adjacent GTM tooling for lead and account management efficiency; define processes to ensure data integrity at funnel entry</p> </li> <li> <p>Partner with Revenue Enablement to train and support revenue teams on top of funnel processes and tooling changes</p> </li> </ul> <h4>What We Look For</h4> <ul> <li> <p>BA/BS or equivalent 4-year degree required</p> </li> <li> <p>2+ years of revenue operations, sales operations, or marketing operations experience</p> </li> <li> <p>Proficiency in Salesforce (experience as an administrator is a plus); familiarity with marketing automation platforms (e.g., Pardot, HubSpot)</p> </li> <li> <p>Mastery of Excel / Google Sheets and a solid understanding of business intelligence tools</p> </li> <li> <p>Experience with lead lifecycle management, lead routing tools (e.g., RingLead, LeanData), data enrichment tools (e.g., Clay, Zoominfo), or lead scoring and attribution frameworks is preferred</p> </li> <li> <p>Proven ability to manage and maintain relationships with key stakeholders in a fast-paced environment, ensuring alignment and engagement throughout projects</p> </li> <li> <p>Prior experience in data analysis, reporting, and funnel performance measurement</p> </li> <li> <p>A strong sense of organization, a growth mentality, and a bias toward documenting and systematizing repeatable processes</p> </li> </ul> <p><br><strong>Pay Grade </strong>- H<br><strong>Equity Grade</strong> - 4<br><br><strong>Employees new to Affirm typically come in at the start of the pay range.</strong> Affirm focuses on providing a simple and transparent pay structure which is based on a variety of factors, including location, experience and job-related skills.<br><br>Base pay is part of a total compensation package that may include equity rewards, monthly stipends for health, wellness and tech spending, and benefits (including 100% subsidized medical coverage, dental and vision for you and your dependents.)<br><br>USA base pay range (CA, WA, NY, NJ, CT) per year: $100,000 - $140,000<br>USA base pay range (all other U.S. states) per year: $88,000 - $128,000<br><br>#LI-Remote</p><div class="content-conclusion"><div> <p>Affirm is proud to be a remote-first company! The majority of our roles are remote and you can work almost anywhere within the country of employment. Affirmers in proximal roles have the flexibility to work remotely, but will occasionally be required to work out of their assigned Affirm office. A limited number of roles remain office-based due to the nature of their job responsibilities.</p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We’re extremely proud to offer competitive benefits that are anchored to our core value of people come first. Some key highlights of our benefits package include:&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Health care coverage - Affirm covers all premiums for all levels of coverage for you and your dependents&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Flexible Spending Wallets - generous stipends for spending on Technology, Food, various Lifestyle needs, and family forming expenses</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Time off - competitive vacation and holiday schedules allowing you to take time off to rest and recharge</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">ESPP - An employee stock purchase plan enabling you to buy shares of Affirm at a discount</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We believe It’s On Us to provide an inclusive interview experience for all, including people with disabilities. We are happy to provide reasonable accommodations to candidates in need of individualized support during the hiring process.</span></p> <p class="p1">[For U.S. positions that could be performed in Los Angeles or San Francisco] Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, Affirm will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.</p> </div> <div> <p>By clicking "Submit Application," you acknowledge that you have read Affirm's&nbsp;<a href="https://www.affirm.com/global-candidate-privacy-notice" target="_blank">Global Candidate Privacy Notice</a>&nbsp;and hereby freely and unambiguously give informed consent to the collection, processing, use, and storage of your personal information as described therein.</p> </div></div>

In addition, the employees may be eligible for equity rewards offered by Affirm Holdings, Inc. (parent company).<br><br>CAN base pay range per year: $78,000 - $118,000<br><br>#LI-Remote</p><div class="content-conclusion"><div> <p>Affirm is proud to be a remote-first company! The majority of our roles are remote and you can work almost anywhere within the country of employment. Affirmers in proximal roles have the flexibility to work remotely, but will occasionally be required to work out of their assigned Affirm office. A limited number of roles remain office-based due to the nature of their job responsibilities.</p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We’re extremely proud to offer competitive benefits that are anchored to our core value of people come first. Some key highlights of our benefits package include:&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Health care coverage - Affirm covers all premiums for all levels of coverage for you and your dependents&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Flexible Spending Wallets - generous stipends for spending on Technology, Food, various Lifestyle needs, and family forming expenses</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Time off - competitive vacation and holiday schedules allowing you to take time off to rest and recharge</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">ESPP - An employee stock purchase plan enabling you to buy shares of Affirm at a discount</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We believe It’s On Us to provide an inclusive interview experience for all, including people with disabilities. We are happy to provide reasonable accommodations to candidates in need of individualized support during the hiring process.</span></p> <p class="p1">[For U.S. positions that could be performed in Los Angeles or San Francisco] Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, Affirm will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.</p> </div> <div> <p>By clicking "Submit Application," you acknowledge that you have read Affirm's&nbsp;<a href="https://www.affirm.com/global-candidate-privacy-notice" target="_blank">Global Candidate Privacy Notice</a>&nbsp;and hereby freely and unambiguously give informed consent to the collection, processing, use, and storage of your personal information as described therein.</p> </div></div>

jobs.title: Procurement Analyst II
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><strong>Who we are</strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 300; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Samsara (NYSE: IOT) is the pioneer of the Connected Operations™ Cloud, which is a platform that enables organizations that depend on physical operations to harness Internet of Things (IoT) data to develop actionable insights and improve their operations. At Samsara, we are helping improve the safety, efficiency and sustainability of the physical operations that power our global economy. Representing more than 40% of global GDP, these industries are the infrastructure of our planet, including agriculture, construction, field services, transportation, and manufacturing — and we are excited to help digitally transform their operations at scale.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 300; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Working at Samsara means you’ll help define the future of physical operations and be on a team that’s shaping an exciting array of product solutions, including Video-Based Safety, Vehicle Telematics, Apps and Driver Workflows, and Equipment Monitoring. As part of a recently public company, you’ll have the autonomy and support to make an impact as we build for the long term.</span></p></div><p><strong>About the role:</strong></p> <p>We are looking for a customer-focused, detail-oriented Procurement Analyst II to join our Procure to Pay team.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>In this role, you will be responsible for providing day-to-day order management and support for the Procure-to-Pay (P2P) processes. By partnering with internal stakeholders and external vendors, you will shape the P2P experience at Samara. As a member of the Procurement team, you will have the opportunity to help drive new initiatives and support our rapidly growing global organization.</p> <p>Above all else, you are focused on delivering a simple procure-to-pay experience that is focused on obsessing over our Customers; building for the long term; and adopting a growth mindset.</p> <p><strong>This is a remote position open to candidates based in Mexico.</strong></p> <p><strong>You should apply if:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>You want to impact the industries that run our world:</strong> Your efforts will result in real-world impact—helping to keep the lights on, get food into grocery stores, reduce emissions, and most importantly, ensure workers return home safely.</li> <li><strong>You are the architect of your own career:</strong> If you put in the work, this role won’t be your last at Samsara. We set up our employees for success and have built a culture that encourages rapid career development, and countless opportunities to experiment and master your craft in a hyper-growth environment.</li> <li><strong>You’re energized by our opportunity:</strong> The vision we have to digitize large sectors of the global economy requires your full focus and best efforts to bring forth creative, ambitious ideas for our customers.</li> <li><strong>You want to be with the best:</strong> At Samsara, we win together, celebrate together and support each other. You will be surrounded by a high-caliber team that will encourage you to do your best.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>In this role, you will facilitate:</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Order Management</li> <ul> <li>Manage purchase requests and complete supplier setups in our ERP.</li> <li>Create Purchase Orders in Netsuite</li> <li>Amend Purchase Orders as instructed by management</li> <li>Partner with Accounts Payable to streamline the Procure to Pay process</li> <li>Drive the continued adoption of our procurement solutions and processes</li> <li>Assist Accounting, where instructed to perform accruals due diligence for the monthly close process</li> </ul> <li>Policy Compliance</li> <ul> <li>Support our procurement compliance processes; purchase requests; purchase approvals; and orders, to ensure audit compliance; high data quality especially around general ledger coding; and all matters impacting accruals</li> <li>Assist in financial control testing when needed</li> </ul> <li>Outreach</li> <ul> <li>Develop deep knowledge of business needs and recommend continuous improvement around procurement processes and systems</li> <li>Challenge our suppliers, employees, and profession to enable a simple Procure to Pay process</li> <li>Partner with Security, Business Technology, and Legal teams to facilitate new supplier screenings</li> <li>Work with our Supplier Community to drive automation and improve supplier performance</li> <li>Manage and maintain the relationship between the internal customers and vendors while focusing on growing positive relationships with our suppliers</li> </ul> <li>Reporting and Analytics</li> <ul> <li>Build and maintain the Procure to Pay dashboard and metrics</li> </ul> </ul> <p><strong>Minimum requirements for the role:</strong></p> <ul> <li>2+ years of experience in Procurement, Supply Chain, or related fields</li> <li>Excellent analytical, verbal, and written skills with a high attention to detail</li> <li>Excellent internal relationship-building skills – your ability to partner with other parts of the organization will be critical to your success</li> <li>Strong customer service skills</li> <li>Familiarity with the budgeting and financial close processes</li> <li>Technical knowledge of financial and procurement systems such as Netsuite, Oracle iProcurement; ZipHQ, or Coupa</li> <li>BA/BS degree or related work experience</li> <li>Fluent in English</li> </ul> <p><strong>An ideal candidate also has:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience working at a publicly traded company is preferred</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Total Rewards</strong></p> <p>At Samsara, we build for the people who keep the global economy moving. We want owners, not passengers, which is why <a href="https://www.samsara.com/company/careers/benefits">our rewards</a> are designed to fuel high-impact builders. Our compensation program delivers above-market total compensation through a combination of base salary, performance-based bonus/variable pay, and equity (for eligible roles) in a high-growth public company. We meaningfully differentiate pay for our top performers, who have the opportunity to earn above-market compensation that can outpace the broader market over time.</p> <p>Beyond compensation, we provide the foundations that enable long-term success: a flexible, employee-led remote model, a professional development stipend, comprehensive health and parental leave plans, and more. If you’re ready to build for the long term and own the outcome, your journey starts here.</p> <p><strong>Flexible Working&nbsp;</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Samsara, we embrace a flexible working model that caters to the diverse needs of our teams. Our offices are open for those who prefer to work in-person and we also support remote work where it aligns with our operational requirements. For certain positions, being close to one of our offices or within a specific geographic area is important to facilitate collaboration, access to resources, or alignment with our service regions. In these cases, the job description will clearly indicate any working location requirements. Our goal is to ensure that all members of our team can contribute effectively, whether they are working on-site, in a hybrid model, or fully remotely. All offers of employment are contingent upon an individual’s ability to secure and maintain the legal right to work at the company and in the specified work location, if applicable.</span></p> <p><strong>Belonging at Samsara</strong></p> <p>At Samsara, we welcome everyone regardless of their background. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, and other characteristics protected by law. We depend on the unique approaches of our team members to help us solve complex problems and want to ensure that Samsara is a place where people from all backgrounds can make an impact.</p> <p><strong>Accommodations&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Samsara is an inclusive work environment, and we are committed to ensuring equal opportunity in employment for qualified persons with disabilities. Please email <a href="mailto:accessibleinterviewing@samsara.com">accessibleinterviewing@samsara.com</a> or <a href="https://forms.gle/DrM6DFQUiSgfPtCVA">click here</a> if you require any reasonable accommodations throughout the recruiting process.</p> <p><strong>Our Commitment to Authenticity</strong></p> <p>We use <a href="https://hiretofu.com/privacyPolicy">Tofu</a>, a fraud detection tool, to validate the authenticity of applications and protect against identity fraud. This ensures we are connecting with real people and allows us to prioritize genuine candidates. Please see Samsara’s <a href="https://www.samsara.com/legal/candidate-privacy-notice">Candidate Privacy Notice</a> for more information.</p> <p><strong>Fraudulent Employment Offers</strong></p> <p>Samsara is aware of scams involving fake job interviews and offers. Please know we do not charge fees to applicants at any stage of the hiring process. Official communication about your application will only come from emails ending in @samsara.com, @us-greenhouse-mail.io or @mail3.guide.co. For more information regarding fraudulent employment offers, please visit our&nbsp;<a href="https://samsara.com/blog/avoiding-fake-job-offers">blog post here</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: Software Engineer, Atlas Growth
jobs.description: <p>Atlas Growth is a cloud engineering group whose mission is to guide customers through their app development journey—from cluster configuration, data modeling and load testing, to running a production workload at scale. We use an in-house experiments platform which helps us validate our features quickly, releasing only the work that positively impacts our customers. Our engineers participate in cross-functional “squads” with product, design, analytics, and research focusing on a single metric (e.g. retention). Our engineering team is part of a larger Atlas Core Engineering org, building foundational elements of MongoDB’s developer data platform.</p> <p>Atlas Growth 2 builds customer-facing features in Atlas and sits alongside other Growth engineering teams. Recent projects include an AI Chatbot for cluster creation, a recommendation system that offers tips for better database performance, and a pricing page designed to optimize conversion rates.</p> <p>This role can be based out of our Dublin office or remotely in Ireland.</p> <h3>Role Overview</h3> <p>Atlas Growth seeks a mid-level software engineer (Software Engineer 3). SE3s are solid contributors to projects they work on and often lead projects of their own. They act in accordance with MongoDB’s core values and leadership principles, and are actively working toward a Senior role.</p> <h3>Candidate Profile</h3> <ul> <li>3+ years of software engineering experience</li> <li>Fluency in TypeScript and JavaScript</li> <li>Proficiency in Java, Go, C++/C, or a similar compiled language</li> <li>Experience writing database queries, either document-based or relational</li> <li>Experience writing and reviewing technical specs, and leading small projects</li> <li>Interest in A/B testing or product design</li> </ul> <h3>Expectations</h3> <ul> <li>Contribute readable and well-tested code to ongoing projects</li> <li>Write scope and technical spec docs for new projects assigned by your manager. Lead small working groups of two or three engineers for these projects</li> <li>Participate actively and constructively in code and document reviews</li> <li>Help product management identify improvements for features you own and drive them to completion</li> <li>Triage incoming support requests and alerts for products you own, and participate in group-wide on-call rotation once onboarded</li> </ul> <h3>About MongoDB</h3> <p>MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform, the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.</p> <p>With offices worldwide and over 60,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.</p> <p>Our compass at MongoDB is our <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company">Leadership Commitment,</a> guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.&nbsp;</p> <p>To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb">From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a>, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a>, and help us make an impact on the world!</p> <p>MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.</p> <p>MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer.</p> <p><em>Req ID: 4263301932</em></p>

jobs.title: Software Engineer, Code Generation
jobs.description: <p>MongoDB is building a world-class team in North America to create tooling that helps customers modernize their applications and migrate their data from legacy relational databases to MongoDB in real-time. As companies modernise legacy workloads and data ecosystems, they are increasingly drawn to the flexibility and scalability of the document model. The tools developed by the Code Generation and Data Migration team are critical in this journey, helping customers with schema modeling, code generation, initial data loads, and continuous data synchronization.</p> <p>We're looking for a Senior Engineer with a strong background in computer science fundamentals, systems design, experience in the Java ecosystem, streaming systems, and data-intensive applications to join our engineering team. In this role, you will be instrumental in designing, building, and optimizing the underlying data structures, algorithms, and database interactions that power our generative AI platform, code generation and migration tools. This involves crafting sophisticated orchestration layers, robust integration points, and high-performance data systems that seamlessly connect and leverage advanced AI capabilities for code generation and&nbsp; building a sophisticated data migration suite using a modern technology stack, which includes Java, Spring Boot, Kafka, Debezium, and React.You will work on critical components that ensure the scalability, efficiency, and reliability of our services, collaborating closely with AI researchers, product management and other engineers to design and implement cutting-edge products that solve complex customer challenges.</p> <p>This role will be based out of North America in the PST and MST zones only.</p> <h3>The ideal candidate for this role will have</h3> <ul> <li>2+ years of&nbsp; engineering experience in backend systems, distributed systems, or core platform development</li> <li>Experience in one or several of Java, Rust, C/C++, and/or Python, with a strong understanding of systems-level programming, memory management, and performance tuning</li> <li>Experience with streaming data platforms such as Apache Kafka and Change Data Capture (CDC) tools like Debezium</li> <li>Experience with relational data modeling and hands-on experience with at least one SQL database (Postgres, MySQL, etc)</li> <li>Exposure to client-side technologies such as JavaScript and React is a plus</li> <li>Good understanding of algorithms, data structures and their time and space complexity</li> <li>Curiosity, a positive attitude, and a drive to continue learning</li> <li>Excellent verbal and written communication skills</li> </ul> <h3>Nice to Have</h3> <ul> <li>Familiarity with cloud-native distributed systems (e.g., Kubernetes)</li> <li>Experience with NoSQL databases and understanding of their trade-offs is great, but not required. We'll teach you NoSQL</li> <li>Contributions to relevant open-source projects</li> </ul> <h3>Position Expectations</h3> <ul> <li>Contribute high-quality, well-tested backend code to the data migration engine and core components of our generative AI orchestration platform</li> <li>Collaborate effectively with Product Management, AI researchers and machine learning engineers and designers to build and deliver on the product roadmap</li> <li>Work to develop robust and efficient backend services that orchestrate AI functionalities</li> <li>Identify and address performance bottlenecks and architectural challenges in our systems, particularly within data flow and orchestration</li> <li>Participate actively in code reviews to enforce best practices and patterns</li> <li>Help troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues in our distributed systems</li> <li>Give and solicit feedback on technical design documents and pull requests</li> <li>Perform tasks related to process such as CI/CD, quality, testing, etc&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h3>Success Measures</h3> <p>Within the first three months, you will have:</p> <ul> <li>Familiarize yourself with the MongoDB database and aggregation language</li> <li>Familiarize yourself with the backend tech stack including Java, Spring Boot, and Kafka</li> <li>Set up software development infrastructure (tech stack, build tools, etc) to enable development using the relevant tech stacks</li> <li>Started collaborating with your peers and contributed to code reviews</li> </ul> <p>Within six months, you will have:</p> <ul> <li>Familiarised yourself with the rest of our the application modernization tool stack</li> <li>Delivered at least one large scale feature that spans the entire tech stack</li> <li>Reviewed and contributed to scope and technical design documents</li> </ul> <p>Within 12 months, you will have:</p> <ul> <li>Become a key contributor to our backend stack, capable of taking on complex features independently</li> <li>Helped recruit and interview new members of the team</li> <li>Collaborated effectively with other teams at MongoDB on cross-functional projects</li> </ul> <h3 class="p1">About MongoDB</h3> <p class="p2">MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform—the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market—helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.</p> <p class="p2">With offices worldwide and nearly 60,000 customers—including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups—relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.</p> <p class="p2">Our compass at MongoDB is our <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company">Leadership Commitment,</a> guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.&nbsp;</p> <p class="p2">To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb">From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a>, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a>, and help us make an impact on the world!</p> <p class="p2">MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.</p> <p class="p2">MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer.</p> <p>REQ ID: 425547</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>MongoDB’s base salary range for this role is posted below. Compensation at the time of offer is unique to each candidate and based on a variety of factors such as skill set, experience, qualifications, and work location. Salary is one part of MongoDB’s total compensation and benefits package. Other benefits for eligible employees may include: equity, participation in the employee stock purchase program, flexible paid time off, 20 weeks fully-paid gender-neutral parental leave, fertility and adoption assistance, Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP) with employer match, mental health counseling, backup child and elder care, and health, dental, and vision benefits offerings. Please note, the base salary range listed below and the benefits in this paragraph are only applicable to candidates based in Canada.</p></div><div class="title">MongoDB’s base salary range for this role in Canada is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$108,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$149,000 CAD</span></div></div></div>

jobs.title: Technical Writer
jobs.description: <p>The Drivers Documentation team is a hands-on and enthusiastic group of engineers who are passionate about teaching developers how to work with data and build applications. We are hiring a “Technical Writer, Drivers” to help us create world-class documentation for MongoDB's developer-focused products -- in particular, MongoDB Drivers and Connectors.</p> <p>Our team works closely with MongoDB's engineering and product organizations to deliver outstanding developer documentation for everyone, regardless of where they are in their software engineering career. Every month, hundreds of thousands of developers turn to our content to help them build microservices, backend and line of business applications, and other projects that are used by millions of people worldwide.</p> <p>We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model.</p> <h3>The team is responsible for the following documentation:&nbsp;</h3> <ul> <li><a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/">MongoDB Drivers</a>, including <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/node/current/">Node</a>, <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/java/sync/current/">Java</a>, <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/go/current/">Go</a>, <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/csharp/current/">.NET/C#</a>, and more!&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/spark-connector/current/">MongoDB Spark Connector</a></li> <li>MongoDB <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/queryable-encryption/">Queryable Encryption</a> and <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/csfle/">Client-Side Field Level Encryption</a></li> </ul> <h3>Candidate Profile:&nbsp;</h3> <p>Candidates must have 3+ years of combined experience in one or more of the following roles: technical writer, developer advocate, and/or&nbsp; software engineer. Beyond industry experience, we are looking for someone who is:</p> <ul> <li>Experienced with developing and shipping modern line-of-business applications OR has extensive experience contributing to complex documentation sites, preferably in a docs-as-code environment</li> <li>Experienced with using great developer documentation and educational materials. You are excited to empower other developers by creating best-in-class documentation</li> <li>Experienced with and enjoys deep investigations of new technical concepts plus teaching others what you’ve learned</li> <li>Eager to keep learning and growing. Passionate about giving and receiving feedback</li> <li>Empathetic with developers of different skill levels and backgrounds to understand what they might find challenging</li> </ul> <h3>Position Expectations:</h3> <ul> <li>Work with technical and non-technical teams to meet the documentation needs of our users</li> <li>Research functionality and features and validate it through testing</li> <li>Develop sample apps &amp; code examples to help users kickstart their projects or refresh themselves on syntax</li> <li>Write documentation for MongoDB <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/">Drivers</a> and <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/tools-and-connectors/#connectors">Connectors</a>. MongoDB develops many official drivers and connectors that users rely on every day</li> <li>Develop tools that help us create and maintain content</li> </ul> <h3>Success Measures</h3> <ul> <li>Within three months, you’ll be comfortable with our tools and writing style and be able to apply them when authoring new content for MongoDB Drivers and Connectors. You’ll also have completed our company’s required technical onboarding.</li> <li>Within six months, you’ll have gained enough product expertise to propose and deliver new projects while building relationships with the teams that we work with. You’ll also be applying our writing style and coding best practices in both your own work and reviews of your teammates’ work.</li> <li>Within twelve months, you’ll have taken ownership of a subject area and be able to estimate and deliver the related work. By this time, you’ll have performed a full annual cycle of performance and growth conversations with your manager.</li> </ul> <h3>About MongoDB</h3> <p>MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform, the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.</p> <p>With offices worldwide and over 60,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.</p> <p>Our compass at MongoDB is our <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company">Leadership Commitment,</a> guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.&nbsp;</p> <p>To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb">From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a>, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a>, and help us make an impact on the world!</p> <p>MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.</p> <p>MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer.</p> <p><em>Req ID: 1273396383</em></p>

jobs.title: Manager II, Engineering - AI Platform Training, Serving and Storage
jobs.description: <p>The AI platform is responsible for all AI infrastructure across Datadog. Our mission is to provide tools and platforms that enable data scientists and engineers to conduct large-scale training and inference with ease. We support products such as <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/datadog-bits-generative-ai/" target="_blank"><u>Bits AI</u></a>, <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/product/llm-observability/" target="_blank"><u>LLMObs</u></a> and all our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/product/llm-observability/" target="_blank"><u>AI research</u></a>.&nbsp;</p> <p>You’ll join a new and fast growing team that is critical to the future of Datadog. You will support building and scaling the team, help define our technical vision and help shape the roadmap. You will manage other managers and help define the future structure of our organization, participating in the recruitment of the future managers and ICs of the department. You’ll work closely with partner teams in the AI platform organization ensuring a seamless AI development cycle. You’ll also partner with the Applied AI org, product engineering teams, and Datadog infrastructure &amp; tooling teams to build out systems from the ground up.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships that it builds, the creativity it brings to the table, and the collaboration of being together. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our employees can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li> <p>Lead a fast-growing organization around 20 people across 2 teams soon to be 3.&nbsp;</p> </li> <li> <p>You will define the roadmap for your scope and work with ICs and managers to establish the technical direction</p> </li> <li> <p>Collaborate with the engineering team and product manager to define the future of the roadmap</p> </li> <li> <p>Create a strong organizational culture centered around our engineering standards and customer- focused approach</p> </li> <li> <p>Support the whole lifecycle of AI development, including model training, serving, deployment and monitoring</p> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li> <p>You’ve led teams that have built 0 to 1 ML/AI Platforms, with an emphasis on Open Source Tooling (i.e. Ray / AnyScale), versus pure out-of-the-box solutions.&nbsp;</p> </li> <li> <p>An experienced engineer and team player with strong technical skills to influence the technical direction of your teams</p> </li> <li> <p>A people leader with strong interpersonal skills, who has built and led high-performing software engineering teams, including managing managers</p> </li> <li> <p>Interested in working on an early stage project with many challenges to solve and a fast iteration cycle. You bring a strong bias for delivery and make impact through ambiguity.</p> </li> <li> <p>You have a track record of delivering high-quality software on schedule and collaborating closely with product partners.&nbsp;</p> </li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. office services globally.&nbsp; This is a critical role within our global Real Estate team.&nbsp; The Real Estate Associate Project Manager will be responsible for all coworking initiatives in Europe and Asia.&nbsp; This includes small real estate project management and real estate administration in a dynamic environment.&nbsp; The role reports to the Vice President, Global Real Estate.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Manage Coworking access&nbsp; for EMEA, APAC as well as backup for AMER and LATAM coworking support to ensure our employees utilizing coworking spaces are fully supported&nbsp;</li> <li>Coordinate EMEA and APAC Coworking floorplans and services with Design &amp; Construction and Office Operations on small projects</li> <li>Complete Weekly Capacity Updates of all Coworking locations where applicable&nbsp;</li> <li>Coordinate with Accounts Payable to update Credit Card information for Coworking and assist with Invoicing Issues</li> <li>Maintain our Datadog Coworking Members summary, tracking all activity including adding/deleting members, expanding/reducing capacity, adding/deleting locations, pay on demand members, rents, etc.</li> <li>Own Global Real Estate Coworking vendor onboarding and Purchase Order creation</li> <li>Monitor and report usage of all Coworking locations in EMEA and APAC</li> <li>Analyze Pay on Demand monthly reporting and make recommendations from that data to maximize efficiency, manage costs, etc.</li> <li>Visit locations as required for site evaluations, location inspections, meet with teams, and to address issues</li> <li>Ensure coworking offices are serviced per the terms of the lease Update all Real Estate Vendor, Landlord Contact Lists and Quarterly Review Materials</li> <li>Drive innovation and continuous improvement by bringing ideas forward to help differentiate the Datadog Real Estate team through efficiency and scalability to operate as, and deliver “best in class” results</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Your experience and qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Minimum of a Degree in Business, Finance, Real Estate or Law&nbsp;</li> <li>Minimum of 2 years experience in real estate, preferably in a fast-growing, international organization</li> <li>Minimum of 2 years financial and/or analytical experience with excellent spreadsheet skills</li> jobs.title: Software Engineer - Early Career
jobs.description: <p>We’re looking for early-career engineers to help us collect, aggregate, visualize, and analyze high-scale metrics, logs, and application data. Software engineering at Datadog includes a variety of exciting opportunities. Interested in distributed systems or Kubernetes across tens of thousands of nodes? Looking to build out products like Real User Monitoring or Cloud Security Management or perhaps join one of our internal teams helping engineering teams ship apps quickly? Want to see your work actually impact and improve the product in a meaningful way? We’ve got you covered. Whether you’re writing a new integration, building data pipelines, or working with the open-source community, our early-career engineers are key contributors to our product.</p> <p>Datadog offers the unique opportunity to work on large-scale, real-time systems by engineers for engineers. Our product is multi-cloud and collects more than a hundred trillion events per day for 28,000+ customers. Early-career engineers work towards the same objectives as their senior teammates to bring lasting impact to our products.</p> <p>We place value in our office culture - the relationships that it builds, the creativity it brings to the table, and the collaboration of being together. Our hybrid workplace ensures our employees can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</p> <p>Students can sign up anytime for a free <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/datadog-github-student-developer-pack/" target="_blank">Datadog Pro account</a>!</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Solve a scaling bottleneck in a critical service</li> <li>Deploy a new feature to production and progressively roll it out with feature flags</li> <li>Investigate and fix a production issue from a service your team owns</li> <li>Create a way to optimize a service to handle more traffic at scale</li> <li>In partnership with your team, plan the most important projects to work on next</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>You are in the process of receiving/have a degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering or related scientific field, or equivalent experience</li> <li>You are targeting a 2026 full-time start date</li> <li>You are comfortable coding in one or more languages&nbsp;</li> <li>You value code simplicity and performance</li> <li>You want to work in a fast-paced environment that values its employees and customers</li> <li>You are a self-starter who enjoys partnering with other engineers and solving difficult problems</li> <li>You’re excited about leveraging AI tools to enhance how you code, solve problems, and build – or eager to learn how</li> <li>You’re looking for or have some exposure using AI coding tools in day-to-day workflows and validate, critique, and refine AI-generated output</li> <li>Bonus: you’re motivated to push the boundaries of how AI can improve software engineering best practices and contribute to building AI-enabled products</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.&nbsp;</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing</li> <li>Intradepartmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, ability to join our Community Guilds (Datadog employee resource groups) and monthly company social events</li> <li>Unlimited snacks and drinks in our office; catered lunches on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday</li> <li>Free, global mental health benefits for employees and dependents age 6+</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong><em>Please ensure that you submit your CV/resume in English. </em></strong><em>If possible, please apply using your personal email address instead of your university email address.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Onsite</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. jobs.title: Technical Account Manager 2 - São Paulo
jobs.description: <p>We are Datadog's in-house product experts. The Technical Solutions team enables Datadog’s worldwide growth by educating potential clients and ensuring that existing customers are happy and successful. As a Technical Account Manager 2 (TAM 2), you’ll serve as a trusted advisor to our strategic customers, accelerating their adoption of the Datadog platform and enabling long-term success. TAM 2s bring deep technical expertise, refined customer skills, and consultative insight into how monitoring, observability, and DevOps practices translate to business value.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture—the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Act as a technical advisor to 3 primary enterprise accounts, ensuring successful product adoption and effective usage of the Datadog platform.</li> <li>Lead enablement and adoption sessions across core product areas tailored to your customer’s architecture and business needs.</li> <li>Analyze customers’ IT Operations environments and workflows to recommend configuration, product usage, and performance improvements.</li> <li>Deliver technical business reviews, health checks, and account maturity assessments, contributing to customer QBRs with impactful recommendations.</li> <li>Escalate product issues appropriately and advocate for your customers’ needs with Datadog’s Product and Engineering teams.</li> <li>Create executive-level summaries and insights that tie platform usage to business outcomes.</li> <li>Participate in internal TAM strategy sessions and contribute to team learning through feature presentations, case studies, or best-practice sharing</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>You have 2+ years of experience in a technical customer-facing role (TAM, Solutions Architect, SRE, DevOps Engineer, or similar) within the cloud or observability space.</li> <li>You’re confident with at least two public cloud platforms (e.g. AWS, Azure, GCP) and have hands-on experience with infrastructure or application monitoring.</li> <li>You’re familiar with scripting or automation tools (e.g. Python, Bash, Terraform, Chef/Puppet).</li> <li>You have working knowledge of CI/CD pipelines and containerization tools (e.g. Jenkins, GitLab, Docker, Kubernetes).</li> <li>You excel at communicating technical concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, and can adapt your style for individual contributors or exec audiences.</li> <li>You’re able to work with minimal guidance on customer enablement, issue triage, and product adoption strategies.</li> <li>You are comfortable traveling up to 25–30% of the time as needed (via auto, air, or train).</li> <li>Previous experience using Datadog or other cloud monitoring tools is a strong plus</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Best-in-breed onboarding program</li> <li>Generous global benefits package</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, including Community Guilds and Inclusion Talks</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on <a href="https://www.instagram.com/datadoghq/?hl=en" target="_blank">Instagram</a>, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/datadog" target="_blank">LinkedIn,</a> and <a href="https://learn.datadoghq.com/" target="_blank">Datadog Learning Center. </a></p> <hr> <p data-renderer-start-pos="215"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is proud to offer <a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf" target="_blank">equal employment opportunity</a> to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law.
